rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-aryl-N-benzyl methacrylamides with CF3SO2Na to afford 3-CF3-substituted pyrrolin-2-ones (3-CF3 oxindole/pyrrolinone-type products)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (assisted by photoexcited Mes-Acr+ photocatalyst under 455 nm irradiation) generates CF3• from CF3SO2Na via SET/desulfonylation; the Pt cathode reduces protons to H2 to close the redox cycle

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from desulfonylation of CF3SO2Na
Carbon-centered radical from CF3 addition to methacrylamide alkene
Cyclized carbocation/enamide-derived intermediate after intramolecular addition onto the N-vinyl group
Mes-Acr• (reduced acridine radical), reoxidized at the anode

electron_flow: Under 455 nm light, Mes-Acr+* oxidizes CF3SO2Na to generate CF3•, which adds to the methacrylamide alkene; the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the pendant enamide and is further oxidized (by Mes-Acr+* or at the anode) to a cation that loses a proton to give the pyrrolinone product, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
notes: Mechanistic section (per TOC) includes electricity on/off experiments (demonstrating need for continuous current/light), cyclic voltammetry studies (to assign oxidation events), and H2 detection tests at the cathode confirming proton reduction as the counter-reaction. PC screening (Table S7) shows Mes-Acr+ClO4- is optimal (81%) and that an added supporting electrolyte (Bu4NBF4, LiClO4) actually lowers yield, suggesting the photocatalyst plus KPF6/Cs2CO3 alone suffice for SET. Detailed radical-trap experiments (TEMPO/BHT) are not visible in the provided pages.
